# Generate a daily temperature matrix based on a modified version of the Logan-Parton model.

Generate a daily temperature matrix based on a modified version of the
Logan-Parton model.

## Usage

``` r
dt_modifiedlp(
  dtr,
  tbar,
  resolution = 1,
  tempdecimalprecision = 1,
  clamp = TRUE
)
```

## Arguments

- dtr:

  Daily temperature range.

- tbar:

  A vector of mean temperatures of the day.

- resolution:

  Temporal resolution of the output (in terms of hours).

- tempdecimalprecision:

  Decimal precision of the resultant temperature.

- clamp:

  Whether to clamp the output values between the minimum and maximum
  values of `tbar`.

## Value

A matrix of `24/resolution` (rows) x `length(tbar)` (cols) containing
temperature values at given times.

## Note

If `length(tbar) == 1`, clamp is automatically set to `FALSE`.

## Modifications

This is a heavily restricted and modified version of the Logan-Parton
model. It is parameterised to a day length of 12 and a sunrise time of
6am.

It makes sure that the mean and median of a given column equals the
associated `tbar`

## References

Parton, W.J. and Logan, J.A., 1981. A model for diurnal variation in
soil and air temperature. Agricultural meteorology, 23, pp.205-216.

## Examples

``` r
dt_modifiedlp(10, tbar = seq(20,45,0.1))
```
